heading: Economic and community development grants
label: 2009aa–2 Economic and community development grants
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (a). , inserted “, Indian Tribes,” after “States” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 118–272, § 2253(e)(1)Subsec. (a)(1). , inserted “, Tribal,” after “State”.Pub. Text read as follows: “Notwithstanding any provision of law limiting the Federal share in any grant program, funds appropriated to carry out this section may be used to increase a Federal share in a grant program, as the Authority determines appropriate.”Pub. children.text: to develop the transportation infrastructure of the region for the purpose of facilitating economic development in the region (except that grants for this purpose may only be made to a State, Tribal, or local government);

to assist the region in obtaining the job training, employment-related education, and business development (with an emphasis on entrepreneurship) that are needed to build and maintain strong local economies;
to provide assistance to severely distressed and underdeveloped areas that lack financial resources for improving basic public services;
to provide assistance to severely distressed and underdeveloped areas that lack financial resources for equipping industrial parks and related facilities; and
to otherwise achieve the purposes of this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: entirely from appropriations to carry out this section;

children.label: B
children.text: in combination with funds available under another Federal or Federal grant program; or

children.label: A
children.text: Basic public infrastructure in distressed counties and isolated areas of distress.

children.label: B
children.text: Transportation infrastructure for the purpose of facilitating economic development in the region.

children.label: C
children.text: Business development, with emphasis on entrepreneurship.

children.label: D
children.text: Job training or employment-related education, with emphasis on use of existing public educational institutions located in the region.
